text.title: Agents Framework Selection — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `agents-framework-selection` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Decision MUST score frameworks on three axes — graph-vs-role, observability stack, deployment target. Single-axis picks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-axis picks (e.g., «LangGraph is popular») produce wrong choices for ~40% of teams; three axes catches it.

rule.statement: Decision record names ≥2 explicitly rejected alternatives with one-sentence rejection reason each.
rule.rationale: Without explicit rejections, the team will re-litigate the decision in 3 months when someone Googles a different framework.

rule.statement: «No framework, just provider SDK» is an allowed answer for small projects. Don't add complexity that doesn't pay rent.
rule.rationale: ~30% of agent projects don't need a framework; forcing one wastes onboarding time and adds dependency risk.

rule.statement: Decision record names the tech lead accountable for revisiting the choice in 6 months.
rule.rationale: Without a named owner, the choice ossifies and never gets revisited even when the framework moves under it.

rule.statement: Framework MUST fit the deployment target (e.g., LangGraph Server vs serverless function vs Anthropic Agent SDK on Cloud Run).
rule.rationale: Frameworks that don't fit the deployment add custom infra; that infra becomes the bottleneck within 6 months.
